prerequisites: An Onfleet account with administrator access to the dashboard, which is required to view and manage API keys.
An Onfleet API key generated from the dashboard's API settings section (used for the required api_key config field).
The Basic Auth password value for the password config field. Onfleet authenticates the API key as the HTTP Basic Auth username with a blank password, so this field is typically left empty.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic authentication)
authMethods.summary: Onfleet authenticates every request via HTTP Basic access authentication. The API key string is sent as the username (the connector's api_key field) and the password is blank (the connector's password field). A valid key must be provided with every request.

setupSteps.title: Generate an Onfleet API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Onfleet dashboard as an administrator and open the API settings section. Create an API key for this integration. You can generate separate keys for different applications and environments.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key into the required api_key field. Onfleet sends this value as the username in HTTP Basic access authentication on every request.

setupSteps.title: Set the Basic Auth password
setupSteps.body: Fill the required password field. Because Onfleet uses the API key as the Basic Auth username with a blank password, this field is normally left empty unless your setup requires a value.
